description_long: This sculpture depicts a standing male figure dressed in classical Roman military attire, including a tunic, armor, and a draped cloak. The figure stands with one leg slightly forward, resting beside a vertical support that resembles a post or tree stump. The facial features are well-defined, with short hair and a stern expression, and the right arm is bent across the chest while the left arm hangs by the side. The back of the sculpture shows the cloak draped over the shoulders and down to the calves, with detailed folds in the fabric. The base is rectangular and solid, providing stability to the figure. The overall surface appears to be stone, with a weathered, matte finish and subtle variations in tone.
description: A classical stone sculpture of a standing male figure in Roman military attire, with a draped cloak and detailed armor, resting beside a vertical support.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed face, armor, and draped cloak, with the right arm bent across the chest and the support post visible beside the left leg.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the draped cloak and the figure's profile, with the support post partially visible behind the leg.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length of the cloak and the back of the support post, with less detail on the figure's features.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the arm position and the edge of the armor, with the support post and base visible.
attribute_subject_matter: human figure in Roman military attire
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
